V-M-081.purpose: Verify VPN device abuse alert creation, dedupe, admin review lifecycle, device-scoped actions, archive behavior, and redaction.

module-checks.check-1: backend/tests/test_vpn_device_abuse_alerts.py covers M-081 service, schema, dedupe, archive, redaction, no-roaming-alert, and explicit action semantics.
module-checks.check-2: backend/tests/test_admin_device_control.py covers M-006/M-024 action boundaries so rotate/block affect exactly one alert device.
module-checks.check-3: backend/tests/test_handshake_anomaly_detector.py and anti-abuse fixtures prove only confirmed ping-pong/multi-network events can feed M-081.
module-checks.check-4: scripts/phase78-vpn-device-abuse-alerts-smoke.mjs statically verifies frontend-admin inbox/archive markers, confirmations, compact layout, and protected-surface guard.

verification-levels.module-local: Service tests prove alert creation, dedupe, no-roaming-alert behavior, safe serialization, and action state transitions.
verification-levels.wave-level: Admin API tests prove auth, confirmation, device-only rotate/block integration, audit-safe details, and archive pagination.
verification-levels.phase-level: Frontend-admin smoke proves open inbox, archive, detail drawer, action confirmations, compact mobile-safe layout, and protected deploy/runtime guard.

scenarios.scenario-1: ping_pong_abuse_detected creates exactly one open alert for the user/device/config context.
scenarios.scenario-2: multi_network_abuse_detected creates or updates one deduplicated alert and increments occurrence_count on repeated evidence.
scenarios.scenario-3: resolve action closes the alert without changing device status or VPN peer state.
scenarios.scenario-4: rotate-device action requires confirmation, rotates only the alert device config, and moves the alert to archive.
scenarios.scenario-5: block-device action requires confirmation, blocks only the alert device, and moves the alert to archive.
scenarios.scenario-6: normal roaming, same endpoint, port-only NAT change, warning, or Redis degraded event must not create an open alert.
scenarios.scenario-7: action without confirmation, wrong admin auth, missing alert, or unrelated device target is rejected.
scenarios.scenario-8: payloads, logs, and audit details must not contain private keys, preshared keys, full VPN configs, bearer tokens, or raw config bodies.
scenarios.scenario-9: ANTI_ABUSE_MODE=observe remains the default and alert creation alone never rotates configs, blocks devices, disables users, or changes deploy/runtime state.

api-contracts.api-1: GET /api/v1/admin/vpn/abuse/alerts?status=open|resolved returns paginated safe alert summaries for admins only.
api-contracts.api-2: GET /api/v1/admin/vpn/abuse/alerts/{alert_id} returns safe user/device/context detail without secrets.
api-contracts.api-3: POST /api/v1/admin/vpn/abuse/alerts/{alert_id}/resolve requires confirmation and closes without enforcement.
api-contracts.api-4: POST /api/v1/admin/vpn/abuse/alerts/{alert_id}/rotate-device requires confirmation and rotates only alert.device_id.
api-contracts.api-5: POST /api/v1/admin/vpn/abuse/alerts/{alert_id}/block-device requires confirmation and blocks only alert.device_id.

data-invariants.invariant-1: dedupe_key is stable for user_id, device_id, signal_type, config_version, and unresolved review state.
data-invariants.invariant-2: source_event_id links back to an M-025 DeviceSecurityEvent when available.
data-invariants.invariant-3: resolved alerts leave the open inbox and remain visible through the resolved/archive query.
data-invariants.invariant-4: migration is additive and must not rewrite existing users, devices, VPN peers, or device security events.

commands.command: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/modules/M-001.xml docs/modules/M-006.xml docs/modules/M-010.xml docs/modules/M-023.xml docs/modules/M-024.xml docs/modules/M-025.xml docs/modules/M-028.xml docs/modules/M-031.xml docs/modules/M-076.xml docs/modules/M-081.xml docs/plans/Phase-78.xml docs/verification/V-M-081.xml

redaction-rules.rule-1: Alert list/detail may include user email, device id/name, config_version, last_endpoint, last_handshake_at, signal_type, severity, occurrence_count, and safe timestamps.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Alert payloads must not include private keys, preshared keys, full config text, bearer tokens, raw secrets, or full device config downloads.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Admin audit details must reference alert id, device id, action, and safe result status only.
redaction-rules.rule-4: Frontend-admin smoke must fail if rendered bundle/source includes private_key, preshared_key, interface private material, bearer token, or raw config markers inside VPN abuse alert UI.

protected-surfaces.surface-1: install.sh, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, MTProto runtime, and public user frontend remain unchanged unless a later approved phase says otherwise.
protected-surfaces.surface-2: VPN config format and create-client.sh behavior remain unchanged; Phase-78 only exposes reviewed admin workflows around existing device config lifecycle.
protected-surfaces.surface-3: MTProto alert and promotion-tag modules remain out of scope.

autonomous-execution-safety.reason: Phase-78 is additive and admin-only, but it touches schema, admin API, device enforcement, and frontend-admin UI; execution must run backend tests, admin build/smoke, XML/MyGRACE gates, and protected-surface guards before closure.

acceptance.item-1: Open VPN abuse alerts are visible in admin UI and resolved alerts remain visible in archive.
acceptance.item-2: Every disruptive action is explicit, confirmed, audited, and device-scoped.
acceptance.item-3: No automatic punishment is introduced; ANTI_ABUSE_MODE remains observe by default.
acceptance.item-4: Protected deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose/MTProto runtime surfaces remain unchanged unless a later approved phase says otherwise.

V-M-081.evidence: PASS local: backend targeted tests 12/12, frontend-admin build, Phase-78 smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and git diff --check passed.
